stdout:
**Completeness Against Criteria**

No blocking failures found.

The issue’s hard criteria are to validate the design decisions and the 12-task dependency-wave breakdown before fan-out. Revision 4 now covers the core decisions coherently: scaffold creates only `C → P`, while breakdown creates `B` and splices the implementation graph into `C → impl → B → P` ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:49]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:49), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:59]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:59), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:176]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:176)). The success criteria explicitly cover scaffold, breakdown-gate blocking, transitive coverage, retrofit, SDD/research examples, skills, and steering scenarios ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:192]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:192)).

The prior hard gap around `child-state = any` is fixed. D13, T3, T6, and the success criteria now specify that preview coverage omits `child-state`, while closure coverage uses `child-state = "done"` ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:118]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:118), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:146]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:146), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:158]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:158), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:197]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:197)).

**Technical Soundness And Architectural Fit**

No blocking failures found.

The revised `child-state` plan matches the current evaluator. `label-coverage` reads `child-state` as optional; absence leaves `state_matcher` as `None`, and `state_ok` then passes for any child state ([crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:557]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:557), [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:581]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:581), [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:590]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:590)). The plan also correctly avoids adding an `"any"` token: current ruleset loading validates configured child states against lifecycle tokens only ([crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:393]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:393), [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1358]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1358), [crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1718]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1718)).

The transitive traversal work is now scoped to `label-coverage`, which is the rule that currently uses direct adjacency through `children_of` ([crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:611]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:611), [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:683]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:683)). The plan correctly leaves `criteria-to-check` and `criteria-label-match` as same-issue rules, matching the current implementations ([crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1065]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1065), [crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1227]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:1227)).

The `validate --scope` design now fits the existing architecture by mirroring transition-time graph enforcement: select applicable graph rules, materialize a narrowed slice, then evaluate graph rules over that slice ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:128]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:128), [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:614]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:614), [crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:722]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/mod.rs:722)). D14 now clearly separates scope membership from coverage-candidate exclusion, so the `type:breakdown` rule can fire while `B` is excluded as a coverer ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:119]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:119)).

Advisory: T4 should preserve the project’s existing repo-relative path safeguards when resolving external plan docs. The storage layer already canonicalizes and containment-checks working-tree document reads ([crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:811]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:811)), and document command paths intentionally pass stored repo-relative paths through that layer ([crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:318]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/document.rs:318)). The plan’s boundary/pure-engine split is compatible with that, but implementers should not bypass it.

**Decomposition And Dependencies**

No blocking failures found.

The 12 tasks are coherent and independently implementable. Wave 0 now contains independent engine primitives; the previously contradictory T2/T3 relationship is removed ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:125]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:125), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:136]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:136)). Wave 1 now has explicit intra-wave ordering: T4 and T6 first, then T5, with T5 depending on T1/T4/T6 because it consumes doc-location and `plan-review` preset behavior ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:153]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:153), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:162]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:162)).

T10 correctly owns creation of `B` and the new spine wiring, while T5 explicitly does not create `B` ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:164]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:164), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:176]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:176)). This fixes the earlier ownership conflict. T10 also correctly states that the existing `breakdown_issue` wiring cannot be reused as-is; the current helper copies parent dependencies to every child and makes the parent depend on every child ([crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:79]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:79), [crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:86]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:86)).

**Risks And Actionability**

No blocking failures found.

The material risks are surfaced with mitigations or decisions. R1 is resolved and matches code: type-hierarchy validation is label-only and does not validate dependency edges ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:211]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:211), [crates/jit/src/type_hierarchy.rs:368]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/type_hierarchy.rs:368)). R2 defines the scoped-validation limitation for repo-wide rules ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:216]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:216)). R5 is no longer a hand-wave: it makes schema regeneration mandatory for the current `.jit/rules.toml` / frozen-schema split ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:223]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:223), [.jit/rules.toml:19]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.jit/rules.toml:19), [.jit/config.toml:24]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.jit/config.toml:24)).

The critical tasks are actionable enough for fan-out. T2 defines slice membership and rule firing semantics; T3 defines traversal, exclusion, and container indirection; T6 defines the gate/rule split; T10 defines the replacement breakdown wiring and gate execution path.

**Prior Review Feedback**

The latest run’s blocking feedback is addressed. The invalid `child-state = any` setting was replaced with omitted `child-state` everywhere it is load-bearing ([dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:10]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:10), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:118]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:118), [dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:197]( /home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md:197)). Earlier blocking items were also resolved: `B` ownership is no longer contradictory, `criteria-label-match` is no longer given traversal behavior, `validate --scope` includes `B` as an evaluation source, and T5 now depends on T4/T6.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
